claude_reasoning.reasoning: Sign convention issue - debt service of 5720 is correctly extracted as positive value representing cash outflow amount. The validation rule expects negative values, but positive values are common in financial models to show absolute amounts.
Sign convention issue - material costs of 1200 is a legitimate expense amount. Many financial models show costs as positive values representing absolute amounts rather than negative cash flows.
Sign convention issue - opex of 3800 is consistent with standard financial modeling where operating expenses are shown as positive amounts.
Sign convention issue - labor costs of 950 follows the same pattern as other expense items, showing absolute cost amounts as positive values.
Sign convention issue - depreciation of 4250 is constant across all periods, indicating this is a legitimate accounting entry shown as positive amount.
Sign convention issue - interest expense of 2520 decreases over time (2520→2240→1960→1680→1400→1120→840→560→280→0), showing realistic debt paydown pattern.
Sign convention issue - tax expense of 8645 increases over time as profits grow, which is economically logical. Positive values represent absolute tax amounts.
Duplicate of flag 0 - same debt service sign convention issue.
FY2027/28 debt service shows expected decrease (5440 vs 5720), consistent with debt amortization schedule.
Material costs show modest inflation (1230 vs 1200), which is realistic for operational projections.
Opex shows inflation adjustment (3876 vs 3800), consistent with multi-year operational model.
DSCR of 10.28 is high but not unrealistic for infrastructure projects in later operational years when debt has been paid down significantly.
DSCR of 11.44 continues the trend of improving coverage ratios as debt decreases and cash flows stabilize.
DSCR of 12.86 follows logical progression - as debt service decreases (4320→4040→3760), coverage ratios naturally increase.
DSCR of 14.47 is high but follows the established pattern of decreasing debt service (3480) with growing EBITDA.
DSCR of 16.34 is very high but mathematically consistent - debt service drops to 3200 while EBITDA reaches 52280. This suggests end of debt term approaching.
